summary: Mechanical rename is internally consistent in src code — all type/schema/field symbols have been updated and imports resolve. However, several test descriptions, JSDoc comments, section banners and feature-spec docs still use the old domain vocabulary, and package.json was not version-bumped despite the commit message promising a 2.0.1 release.

suggestions.description: Test description still says "accepts full dev data" inside the renamed describe("engineeringDataSchema") block.
suggestions.rationale: The point of this rename is to unify vocabulary on the new domain names. A test that says "dev data" under a describe named "engineeringDataSchema" will confuse future readers and grep hunts for leftover references. The rename is incomplete.

suggestions.description: Test descriptions still say "full quality data" and "minimal quality data (score only)" inside the renamed describe("codeQualityDataSchema") block.
suggestions.rationale: Same issue as above — new naming should be consistent end-to-end. The fix target was domain vocabulary, not just symbol names.
suggestions.suggestion: Rename to `"accepts full code quality data"` and `"accepts minimal code quality data (score only)"` (lines 299, 311).

suggestions.description: JSDoc on toWorkEntities still reads "for the v2 Push payloads project schema" but the schema is now called "work".
suggestions.rationale: The function was renamed `toProjectEntities` → `toWorkEntities` but the comment describing what it does still uses the old term. Readers following the doc will expect a `project` field on the payload and be unable to find it.
suggestions.suggestion: Change comment to: "for the v2 Push payloads work schema."

suggestions.description: Inconsistent section-header comments after rename: "-- Core envelope (Task 1) --" still carries the historical task number while "-- Schema: \"work\" --", "-- Schema: \"engineering\" --", "-- Schema: \"product\" --", "-- Schema: \"codeQuality\" --" all dropped their task numbers (previously (Task 3/4/5/6)).
suggestions.rationale: Either strip all `(Task N)` markers or keep them. The half-rename leaves the file visually inconsistent and the remaining `(Task 1)` is now orphaned context with no companions.
suggestions.suggestion: Drop `(Task 1)` from line 219 (and any other lingering task-number markers) for consistency with the other section banners.

suggestions.description: Canonical client documentation still lists old type names: "ProjectSchema, ProjectEntity, DevSchema, DevTask, DevReview, PMSchema, PMUpdate, QualitySchema".
suggestions.rationale: These docs are the public-facing reference for what types the package exports. Leaving the old names here will point users at symbols that no longer exist in `@magic-ingredients/no-tickets-client/types`. Future contributors will also mistake the old names for current API.
suggestions.suggestion: Update line 66 to list the new names: `WorkSchema, WorkEntity, EngineeringSchema, EngineeringTask, EngineeringReview, ProductSchema, ProductUpdate, CodeQualitySchema`.

suggestions.description: Feature doc references `toProjectEntities()` and `ProjectEntity array` — both symbols no longer exist (lines 59 and 93).
suggestions.rationale: The CLI feature doc claims the CLI uses `toProjectEntities()` to produce a `ProjectEntity array`. The code now uses `toWorkEntities()` producing `WorkEntity[]`. The doc is now a lie.

suggestions.description: Fix document Task 1 explicitly requires: "Keep backward-compat type aliases for any types exported via the public SDK sub-path." No such aliases exist in the commit.
suggestions.rationale: The published types are exposed at `./types` and `./schemas` sub-path exports (see package.json). Task 1 of the fix spec mandates aliases there. The commit message justifies skipping them ("no consumers of 2.0.0 yet"), which may be fine operationally — but the fix document still states aliases are required. Either the fix spec must be updated to reflect the new decision, or aliases must be added. Leaving the contradiction unresolved means the fix cannot be marked `completed` truthfully.
suggestions.suggestion: Either (a) add deprecated type aliases in `src/core/types.ts` and `src/core/schemas.ts` (e.g. `export type ProjectSchema = WorkSchema`), or (b) amend the fix markdown to remove the back-compat requirement and record the rationale in the resolution block.

suggestions.description: Package version is still 2.0.0 despite the commit message promising release as 2.0.1.
suggestions.rationale: The field-level rename (`project` → `work`, etc.) is a breaking wire-format change. The commit message rationalises a patch bump on grounds that there are no consumers yet, but the version field was not bumped in this commit. Publishing 2.0.0 with changed payload shape would silently break anyone who did pick up 2.0.0. Even under the stated plan, the bump needs to happen.
suggestions.suggestion: Bump `version` to `2.0.1` (or `2.1.0` / `3.0.0` per strict semver for the breaking wire change) in `package.json` before release.

suggestions.description: Test name `omits work schema when no files provided` — reads slightly awkwardly since the field is `work`, not a "work schema". Consider `omits work data when no files provided`.
suggestions.rationale: Minor polish. The field is literally named `work`, and referring to it as "work schema" blurs the distinction between the Zod schema object and the payload field.
suggestions.suggestion: Rename to `omits work field when no files provided` or similar.
